title: FLS Module 4: Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which ASA (American Society of Anesthesiologists) classification corresponds to a patient with a severe systemic illness that limits activity but is not life-threatening?

answerOptions.text: A) ASA 2
B) ASA 3
C) ASA 4
D) ASA 5

questions.explanation: ASA 3 denotes a patient with severe systemic disease that limits activity but is not incapacitating. ASA 4 refers to life-threatening conditions, and ASA 5 is for moribund patients. ASA 2 indicates mild systemic disease.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following should always be discussed with the patient during the initial preoperative consultation for laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Details about trocar types and camera angle
B) The surgeon’s estimated operating time
C) Possibility of conversion to open surgery
D) Use of advanced energy devices

questions.explanation: It is essential that patients are informed about all possible outcomes, including the potential need to convert to an open procedure, during the informed consent process.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is considered a relative contraindication to laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Inability to tolerate laparotomy
B) Uncorrectable hypovolemic shock
C) Lack of surgical training
D) Previous abdominal surgery

questions.explanation: While prior abdominal surgery increases the risk of adhesions, it is not an absolute contraindication. The other choices represent absolute contraindications, such as uncorrectable hypovolemic shock or inadequate surgical training.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is not an absolute contraindication to laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Lack of facility resources for perioperative care
B) Severe cardiopulmonary disease
C) Unresuscitated hypovolemic shock
D) Bowel obstruction with dilated loops

questions.explanation: Although bowel obstruction is a challenge, it may still be approached laparoscopically depending on the clinical scenario. The other options are absolute contraindications due to significant safety concerns.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: What is a recommended strategy for initial access in thin or muscular patients to avoid vascular injury?

answerOptions.text: A) Use of high insufflation pressures
B) Veress needle insertion directly through the umbilicus
C) Open access or optical trocar technique away from midline
D) Blind trocar insertion through previous scars

questions.explanation: In thin or muscular patients, the aortoiliac vessels are closer to the abdominal wall, increasing injury risk. Using an open or optical technique away from midline helps reduce this risk.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: In a patient with cirrhosis undergoing laparoscopy, which complication is of particular concern?

answerOptions.text: A) Hypercapnia
B) Ascitic fluid leakage through trocar sites
C) Ileus
D) Deep vein thrombosis

questions.explanation: Cirrhotic patients often have ascites, which can leak through trocar sites postoperatively. Hemorrhage risk is also elevated due to coagulopathy.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which patient condition most strongly warrants avoiding blind initial trocar placement at the umbilicus?

answerOptions.text: A) Pregnancy in the first trimester
B) Previous appendectomy
C) Umbilical hernia repair with mesh
D) Prior gastric bypass surgery

questions.explanation: Blind trocar or Veress needle placement through previously placed mesh increases the risk of bowel or vascular injury. Access should be performed under direct vision or in an alternate location.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following statements is true regarding laparoscopy during pregnancy?

answerOptions.text: A) It is contraindicated in the third trimester
B) It can be performed in any trimester with proper precautions
C) Only open surgery should be performed in pregnant patients
D) Fetal heart monitoring is not necessary perioperatively

questions.explanation: Laparoscopy is safe in all trimesters when done by experienced teams with adjustments like lateral positioning, lower insufflation pressures, and fetal monitoring.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following conditions is often mistaken as a contraindication but is not a contraindication to laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Portal hypertension in laparoscopic cholecystectomy
B) COPD
C) Inability to resuscitate from shock
D) Lack of appropriate team experience

questions.explanation: COPD was previously thought to be a contraindication, but with appropriate perioperative management, these patients can safely undergo laparoscopy.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which precaution is appropriate when planning laparoscopy in a patient with known visceral arterial aneurysm?

answerOptions.text: A) Use of higher insufflation pressure to tamponade the aneurysm
B) Preference for blind Veress needle insertion
C) Awareness of increased risk for vascular injury during access
D) Laparoscopy is contraindicated in all cases

questions.explanation: Aneurysms increase the risk of bleeding during entry. Preoperative knowledge allows the surgeon to adjust the access technique to minimize danger.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: What is the primary reason for continuing cardiac and pulmonary medications perioperatively?

answerOptions.text: A) They increase anesthetic depth
B) They promote faster extubation
C) They maintain baseline organ function and reduce risk
D) They interact synergistically with muscle relaxants

questions.explanation: Cardiac and pulmonary medications help maintain physiologic stability and should generally be continued unless contraindicated.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following supplements is known to affect platelet function and should be reviewed preoperatively?

answerOptions.text: A) Folic acid
B) Vitamin C
C) Fish oil
D) Calcium

questions.explanation: Fish oil and other supplements like ginkgo and garlic may impair platelet function and increase bleeding risk.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which positioning precaution is recommended for a pregnant patient undergoing laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Supine with no tilt
B) Right lateral recumbent decubitus
C) Left lateral decubitus
D) Steep Trendelenburg

questions.explanation: This position helps reduce uterine compression of the vena cava, improving venous return and minimizing hypotension during pregnancy.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: What is the most important aspect of a successful informed consent process?

answerOptions.text: A) Using detailed medical terminology
B) Providing a copy of the hospital’s consent policy
C) Ensuring a two-way dialogue with time for patient questions
D) Having a witness sign the consent form

questions.explanation: Informed consent is a dynamic, bidirectional process requiring adequate explanation and patient understanding.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: What is the appropriate perioperative consideration for a patient on long-term corticosteroid therapy?

answerOptions.text: A) Discontinue steroids 24 hours before surgery
B) Delay surgery for 6 weeks after stopping steroids
C) Administer stress-dose steroids perioperatively
D) Reduce steroid dose postoperatively to prevent adrenal crisis

questions.explanation: Chronic steroid use suppresses the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis. Stress-dose steroids are recommended to reduce the risk of adrenal insufficiency during the perioperative period.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is a common method used to enhance patient education before laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Requiring patients to attend group classes
B) Mailing operative reports from prior surgeries
C) Providing procedure-specific booklets and/or videos
D) Reviewing surgical anatomy in detail during consent

questions.explanation: Many surgeons offer brochures or videos to help patients better understand the procedure and postoperative expectations.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: In obese patients undergoing laparoscopy, which technique can assist in safe secondary trocar placement?

answerOptions.text: A) Use of a Foley catheter
B) Placement of a spinal needle to mark the path
C) Lateral decubitus positioning
D) Pre-insufflation with oxygen

questions.explanation: In obese patients, finger palpation may be unreliable. Using a spinal needle can help define safe points of trocar entry.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: When should a surgeon consider converting from a laparoscopic to an open approach in a patient with bowel obstruction?

answerOptions.text: A) When insufflation pressure exceeds 12 mmHg
B) If the obstruction is caused by a hernia
C) When visualization is limited by massively distended bowel
D) Whenever adhesions are present

questions.explanation: Large bowel dilation limits safe visualization and increases risk of enterotomy. Conversion may be needed to manage this safely.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is a relative contraindication specific to laparoscopic appendectomy?

answerOptions.text: A) Prior cesarean section
B) Presence of a phlegmon or large abscess
C) Mild leukocytosis
D) Right lower quadrant pain for less than 24 hours

questions.explanation: Large abscesses or phlegmons may require drainage or conversion to open procedure and present challenges to safe laparoscopy.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Why is it important to evaluate the institution’s capacity and OR team expertise before deciding on a laparoscopic approach?

answerOptions.text: A) To ensure the case duration is under 30 minutes
B) To guarantee patient satisfaction
C) Because local capabilities directly affect patient safety and outcomes
D) Because surgeons need to meet billing benchmarks

questions.explanation: Institutional resources, surgical team experience, and support services influence whether laparoscopy can be performed safely.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is true about laparoscopy in trauma?

answerOptions.text: A) It is the gold standard for all abdominal trauma
B) It should only be used when hemodynamic instability is present
C) It is most useful in stable patients for evaluating diaphragm or bowel injury
D) It is contraindicated after penetrating injuries

questions.explanation: Laparoscopy plays a role in diagnosing occult injuries in stable trauma patients. It helps avoid unnecessary laparotomies.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which preoperative finding increases concern for adhesions despite absence of visible abdominal scars?

answerOptions.text: A) Smoking history
B) History of peritonitis
C) BMI <20
D) Prior endoscopy

questions.explanation: Peritonitis causes adhesions even if no surgical incisions are present. This raises concern for injury during initial access.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Why is it critical to avoid Veress needle insertion through a previous umbilical hernia repair site?

answerOptions.text: A) It causes increased insufflation rates
B) Risk of bowel or mesh injury is high
C) The scar is too rigid for entry
D) Cosmetic outcomes are compromised

questions.explanation: Blind entry through repaired hernia sites risks damage to prosthetic mesh and underlying structures.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment

questions.text: Which of the following is recommended when assessing a patient preoperatively for laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Routine pulmonary function tests in all patients
B) Performing treadmill testing for anesthesia clearance
C) Reviewing most recent imaging, lab values, and EKG
D) Scheduling surgery before any medical clearance is complete

questions.explanation: A thorough pre-op assessment includes review of relevant labs, imaging, and cardiopulmonary clearance when appropriate.
questions.submodule: Module 4 – Patient Selection and Preoperative Assessment
